evals.prompt: dad just sent me a bunch of app specs, can you figure out where these go? they're in <DOWNLOADS>/New App Specs/
evals.expected_output: Should read all 7 files, identify at least 3-4 distinct app concepts, detect App3_App4 needs splitting, check existing projects, propose new projects where needed, present detailed assessment.
expectations: Assessment reads and summarizes all 7 files from the directory
Assessment identifies at least 3 distinct app concepts (Recipe/Kitchen Ops, Culinary Cottages Owner Portal, Reservation Manager)
Assessment detects that App3_App4_Placeholders.md covers multiple topics and proposes splitting it
Assessment checks existing vault projects before proposing new ones
Assessment maps each file or group to a specific project path with reasoning
Assessment distinguishes between materials that fit existing projects vs. needing new projects
Assessment proposes full project scaffolding for any new projects (agents.md, lessons.md, specs/, plans/, reports/)
Assessment suggests next steps for each project (e.g., create-spec for detailed materials, needs more info for placeholders)

evals.prompt: Christian sent over some notes about the Odoo BI field mappings for the signal engine, here: <DOWNLOADS>/odoo-bi-fields.md — can you file this somewhere useful?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this relates to signal-engine and/or work-for-dad. Should check candidate projects' agents.md files. Should present reasoned assessment.
expectations: Assessment reads the full content of odoo-bi-fields.md
Assessment identifies signal-engine ({{ORG}} Intelligence) as a candidate project
Assessment checks at least 2 candidate projects before recommending placement
Assessment explains why the chosen project is the best fit
Assessment proposes placing the file in a source subfolder (e.g., Christian Files/) within the target project
Assessment does NOT propose creating a new project for a single reference file that fits an existing one

evals.prompt: I've got a folder of research PDFs and notes about competitor reservation systems — SevenRooms alternatives, OpenTable API docs, and a few restaurant tech articles. They're at ~/Downloads/reservation-research/. File these for me.
evals.expected_output: Should identify ambiguity in placement, consider multiple candidate projects, and present reasoning. Should flag uncertainty and ask for clarification.
expectations: Assessment reads all files in the reservation-research directory
Assessment identifies the ambiguity — these could fit multiple projects
Assessment considers at least 2 candidate locations (e.g., Restaurant Ops and a potential new reservation project)
Assessment explains the trade-offs of each placement option
Assessment flags uncertainty about placement and indicates it would ask the user to clarify
Assessment suggests next steps appropriate to the material's readiness level
